meaning: n.鎺ㄦ枼,鎺掓枼,涓ユ嫆,鍘屾伓,鍙嶉┏
examples: She gave a dramatic shudder of repulsion.
The real killer is that if we get too close, we're even going to have nuclear-nuclear repulsion between the nuclei of the two atoms.
This is the Repulsion Phase of the Power Cycle.
It cannot be separated from what the object of our repulsion means to us.
There are tantalising allusions to both the fascination and the repulsion which animated the later Ottomans' ambivalence towards Europe.
